title: Full refactor of a mixed-style UserService module
prompt: Refactor test_fixtures/mixed_style_module.ex into lib/my_app/accounts/user_service.ex. The input has 6 functions written in Ruby/Java style: if/case chains, defensive is_nil/nil checks, manual loop accumulators, raw Map.get/Map.fetch, no pipe operator, no guards. Rewrite EVERY function to use: multi-clause function heads for dispatch, guard clauses for narrowing, pipe operator for sequencing, with expressions for multi-step error handling, pattern-matched destructure in function heads (no Map.get), and Enum.* where appropriate. Preserve the public API (same function names, same return types) and the module attribute @default_role. Do not introduce nil checks. Do not keep a case statement unless it's genuinely the only option.
tier_rationale: The single broadest refactor challenge in the family — 6 capabilities must all be applied across one module in a coherent rewrite. Sonnet typically fixes the obvious stuff but leaves at least 2-3 imperative patterns behind.
research_citation: BoothIQ post-mortem: 'Claude writes Ruby-style Elixir — if/then/else chains, defensive nil-checking'
